channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs 2026: Fully Transform Your Business Journey Through Europe’s Leading Entrepreneur Exchange Programme
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Starting a business can be exciting, but it can also be overwhelming without the right guidance, networks, and international exposure. For aspiring entrepreneurs and newly established business owners looking to gain practical experience, expand globally, and build valuable partnerships, the Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs (EYE) Programme offers one of the best opportunities in Europe. The […]

The post Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs 2026: Fully Transform Your Business Journey Through Europe’s Leading Entrepreneur Exchange Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Starting a business can be exciting, but it can also be overwhelming without the right guidance, networks, and international exposure. For aspiring entrepreneurs and newly established business owners looking to gain practical experience, expand globally, and build valuable partnerships, the Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs (EYE) Programme offers one of the best opportunities in Europe.

The Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme is a cross-border business exchange initiative supported by the European Union. It gives new and aspiring entrepreneurs the opportunity to learn directly from experienced entrepreneurs who are already running successful small and medium-sized businesses across participating countries.

Whether you are planning to launch your first startup, scale your current business, enter new international markets, or gain fresh business insights, this programme provides hands-on entrepreneurial learning through real-life business exchanges.

With thousands of successful exchanges already completed, the programme continues to empower entrepreneurs by helping them acquire practical business management skills, international experience, networking opportunities, and long-term collaborations.

What is the Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme?

The Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme is a European business exchange programme designed to support entrepreneurship, innovation, and international business collaboration.

Through the programme:

  • New entrepreneurs travel to another participating country
  • They work alongside experienced host entrepreneurs
  • They gain practical business knowledge and mentorship
  • They learn how successful SMEs operate internationally
  • They build long-term professional partnerships

Unlike traditional academic programmes, EYE focuses on practical entrepreneurial experience. Participants work directly with established business owners and learn through day-to-day business operations.

The exchange experience helps entrepreneurs strengthen their business ideas while exposing them to international business environments and new markets.

Programme Statistics and Impact

The Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme has achieved remarkable milestones over the years. Current programme figures include:

  • 14,940 business exchanges completed
  • 27,769 applications from new entrepreneurs
  • 15,994 applications from host entrepreneurs
  • 45 participating countries involved

These numbers demonstrate the growing global relevance and impact of the programme within the entrepreneurial ecosystem.

Key Benefits of the Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme

Participating entrepreneurs gain access to numerous personal and professional development opportunities.

Benefits for New Entrepreneurs

New entrepreneurs can benefit from:

1. Practical Business Training

Participants gain hands-on entrepreneurial knowledge by working directly with experienced business owners.

This includes learning about:

  • Business operations
  • Marketing strategies
  • Financial management
  • Customer acquisition
  • Business expansion
  • Product development
  • International trade

2. International Exposure

The programme enables entrepreneurs to gain international business experience in another participating country.

This helps participants:

  • Understand foreign markets
  • Learn international business practices
  • Develop cross-cultural communication skills
  • Build international confidence

3. Networking Opportunities

Participants build long-term professional relationships with:

  • Experienced entrepreneurs
  • International business partners
  • Industry professionals
  • Potential investors
  • Future collaborators

4. Access to New Markets

Entrepreneurs can explore opportunities for business expansion into international markets while understanding customer behavior and business trends abroad.

5. Business Growth and Innovation

Exposure to new business environments often inspires innovative ideas, improved business models, and fresh strategies that participants can implement in their own ventures.

6. Mentorship from Experienced Entrepreneurs

One of the strongest aspects of the programme is direct mentorship from successful business owners who share practical insights and guidance.

Benefits for Host Entrepreneurs

Experienced entrepreneurs also benefit significantly from participating in the programme.

Host entrepreneurs gain:

  • Fresh perspectives on their businesses
  • Innovative ideas from new entrepreneurs
  • International business connections
  • Opportunities for future collaboration
  • Access to emerging markets
  • Cross-border partnerships
  • Enhanced business innovation

The programme creates a mutually beneficial environment where both parties learn and grow together.

Who Can Participate?

The Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme is open to both new entrepreneurs and experienced entrepreneurs.

New Entrepreneurs

You may qualify if you:

  • Are firmly planning to start your own business
  • Have already started a business within the last three years
  • Want to gain entrepreneurial skills and international experience
  • Are motivated to collaborate with experienced business owners

Experienced Entrepreneurs

You may qualify if you:

  • Own or manage a Small or Medium-Sized Enterprise (SME)
  • Operate a successful business in one of the participating countries
  • Are willing to mentor new entrepreneurs
  • Want to expand your international business network

Participating Countries

The programme currently involves 45 participating countries, providing entrepreneurs with broad international exchange opportunities across Europe and partner countries.

Participants can explore diverse business environments, industries, and entrepreneurial ecosystems while building international collaborations.

Financial Information

One major advantage of the programme is that:

There are no participation fees for the Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme.

This makes the opportunity accessible to entrepreneurs who want international business exposure without expensive programme costs.

Why This Programme Matters for Entrepreneurs

Many startups fail due to lack of mentorship, experience, and market understanding. The Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme addresses these challenges by connecting emerging entrepreneurs with experienced business leaders.

The programme helps bridge the gap between entrepreneurial ambition and practical business execution.

Participants leave the programme with:

  • Improved entrepreneurial confidence
  • Practical management skills
  • International exposure
  • Valuable professional networks
  • Better understanding of global business environments
  • Enhanced growth potential for their businesses

Success Stories from the Programme

The programme has supported numerous successful entrepreneurs across Europe and beyond.

Highlighted entrepreneurs and finalists include:

  • Niko Bucalo (Croatia)
  • Ignacio Márquez Collazos
  • Nuria Álvarez Rodrigo
  • Marco Filippi
  • Guygy Yilmaz Koksal
  • Trayana Tinkova (Bulgaria)
  • Fernando Chico (Spain)

These success stories showcase the programme’s impact in helping entrepreneurs build sustainable businesses and international partnerships.

How to Apply

Interested participants can apply through the official Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs platform.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Prepare a strong business idea or business profile
  2. Clearly define their entrepreneurial goals
  3. Select suitable host entrepreneurs or exchange opportunities
  4. Submit their applications through the official programme portal

CLICK HERE TO APPLY

Contact Information

  • Email: support@erasmus-entrepreneurs.eu
  • Phone: +32 2 282 08 73

Final Thoughts

The Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs Programme remains one of the most valuable international entrepreneurship exchange initiatives available today.

For aspiring entrepreneurs, it offers practical learning, mentorship, international exposure, and long-term business opportunities. For experienced entrepreneurs, it creates opportunities for innovation, collaboration, and market expansion.

If you are serious about building a successful business, expanding internationally, and learning directly from experienced entrepreneurs, this programme could become a major turning point in your entrepreneurial journey.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Grant Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Erasmus for Young Entrepreneurs 2026: Fully Transform Your Business Journey Through Europe’s Leading Entrepreneur Exchange Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Google DeepMind Gemini for Developers Specialization 2026: Become an AI App Builder with Gemini API and Google AI Studio
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming industries around the world, and developers with practical AI application skills are becoming some of the most sought-after professionals in the global tech ecosystem. To help developers build production-ready AI solutions, Google DeepMind has launched the Gemini for Developers Specialization on Coursera — an advanced learning program designed to equip […]

The post Google DeepMind Gemini for Developers Specialization 2026: Become an AI App Builder with Gemini API and Google AI Studio appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming industries around the world, and developers with practical AI application skills are becoming some of the most sought-after professionals in the global tech ecosystem. To help developers build production-ready AI solutions, Google DeepMind has launched the Gemini for Developers Specialization on Coursera — an advanced learning program designed to equip learners with hands-on experience in building AI-powered applications using the Gemini API and Google AI Studio.

This comprehensive 3-course specialization provides aspiring AI developers, software engineers, and technology professionals with the technical expertise needed to architect, deploy, and optimize intelligent AI systems capable of thinking, searching, reasoning, and acting autonomously.

The program is flexible, beginner-accessible for developers with coding experience, and focused heavily on real-world projects that mirror modern AI engineering workflows used across the industry today.

What is the Google DeepMind Gemini for Developers Specialization?

The Gemini for Developers Specialization is an online career-focused learning program offered by Google DeepMind through Coursera. The specialization focuses on practical AI application development using the Gemini ecosystem, including:

  • Gemini API
  • Google AI Studio
  • AI agents
  • Function calling
  • Structured outputs
  • Cloud deployment
  • AI orchestration
  • Cost optimization
  • Prompt engineering
  • Autonomous AI workflows

The specialization is designed to transform developers into AI Application Developers capable of building sophisticated systems that solve real-world problems autonomously.

Learners gain both theoretical understanding and hands-on practical experience through project-based learning.

Why This Specialization Matters in 2026

As companies increasingly adopt generative AI solutions, there is growing demand for developers who can:

  • Build AI-powered applications
  • Deploy scalable AI systems
  • Optimize AI infrastructure costs
  • Create intelligent AI agents
  • Integrate APIs and external tools
  • Automate workflows using AI

Google DeepMind’s specialization directly addresses these industry demands by teaching practical production-level AI engineering skills.

This specialization goes beyond simple AI prompting. It teaches learners how to build complete AI ecosystems that can interact with external tools, process web data, reason transparently, and deliver structured outputs suitable for enterprise applications.

What You Will Learn

The specialization provides comprehensive training in advanced AI application development.

Core Learning Outcomes

Participants will learn how to:

  1. Architect and deploy advanced AI agents using function calling and structured outputs.
  2. Implement strategic model selection for production-ready applications.
  3. Optimize AI application performance and token usage costs.
  4. Build AI-powered systems using Gemini API and Google AI Studio.
  5. Deploy applications to the cloud for scalable production performance.
  6. Use JSON schema and structured outputs for reliable AI workflows.
  7. Integrate Google Search grounding into AI applications.
  8. Build autonomous processors capable of handling complex tasks.
  9. Develop asynchronous AI operations and research assistants.
  10. Monitor cloud deployments and analyze token economics.

Skills Participants Will Gain

Learners completing the program will develop highly marketable AI and software development skills, including:

  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Generative AI Agents
  • Natural Language Processing
  • Cloud API Integration
  • Distributed Computing
  • Prompt Engineering
  • AI Orchestration
  • LLM Application Development
  • Microservices
  • Program Development

These are some of the most in-demand technical skills globally in 2026.

Tools and Technologies Covered

Participants will gain hands-on experience with industry-standard AI development tools such as:

  • Gemini API
  • Google Gemini
  • Google AI Studio
  • LangChain
  • Microsoft Azure
  • Prompt Engineering Tools
  • Agentic Workflows
  • AI Orchestration Systems

The specialization also introduces learners to modern AI workflow engineering practices used by leading technology companies.

Program Structure

The specialization consists of 3 courses:

1. Getting Started with Google Gemini API

Duration: 4 Hours

This introductory course focuses on:

  • Gemini API fundamentals
  • Text generation
  • API integration
  • Initial AI application development

2. Exploring AI with Google AI Studio

Duration: 4 Hours

Participants learn:

  • Prompt optimization
  • AI Studio experimentation
  • Model parameter tuning
  • Rapid prototyping workflows

3. Gemini Capstone Project

Duration: 4 Hours

The final capstone project allows learners to:

  • Build complete AI applications
  • Deploy AI systems to Cloud Run
  • Monitor token usage
  • Create production-ready AI solutions

Hands-On Applied Learning Experience

One of the strongest aspects of this specialization is its focus on real-world project development.

Participants will progressively build:

  • Functional text generators
  • Intelligent AI processors
  • AI research assistants
  • Autonomous AI agents
  • Cloud-deployed AI applications

The projects simulate actual industry workflows used by professional AI developers today.

By the end of the specialization, learners will have a portfolio demonstrating practical AI engineering capabilities that can be showcased to employers, clients, or startup investors.

Advanced AI Features Covered

The specialization dives into several advanced AI engineering concepts, including:

Function Calling

Learners will understand how AI systems can interact with:

  • External tools
  • APIs
  • Databases
  • Services
  • Web search systems

Structured Output with JSON Schema

Participants will learn how to:

  • Generate parseable AI outputs
  • Improve application reliability
  • Build scalable backend integrations

Model Thinking Parameters

Google Gemini’s thinking features help developers:

  • Improve transparent reasoning
  • Enhance output quality
  • Build explainable AI workflows

Grounding with Google Search

This allows AI applications to:

  • Retrieve real-time web information
  • Improve contextual accuracy
  • Deliver updated responses

Cloud Deployment and Production Readiness

The specialization emphasizes production deployment rather than simple experimentation.

Participants will learn:

  • Cloud Run deployment
  • Cost monitoring
  • Token usage optimization
  • Production scalability
  • High-performance AI system management

These are critical skills for developers seeking careers in AI engineering and enterprise AI deployment.

Who Should Apply?

This specialization is ideal for:

  • Software developers
  • AI engineers
  • Backend developers
  • Full-stack developers
  • Cloud engineers
  • Machine learning enthusiasts
  • Tech professionals transitioning into AI
  • Developers building AI startups
  • Students with coding experience

Prerequisites

To succeed in the program, participants should have:

  • Experience with Python or JavaScript
  • Understanding of object-oriented programming
  • Basic REST API knowledge
  • Comfort with debugging code
  • Fundamental software development skills

The specialization is categorized as Intermediate Level.

Certification Benefits

Upon successful completion, learners receive a shareable career certificate from Google DeepMind.

Benefits include:

  • Adding credentials to LinkedIn profiles
  • Strengthening resumes and CVs
  • Demonstrating AI engineering expertise
  • Improving employability
  • Showcasing practical AI development skills

The certificate can help participants stand out in the competitive global AI job market.

Flexible Learning Schedule

The specialization is designed for flexibility.

Duration

  • Approximately 4 weeks
  • Around 3 hours per week

Learning Format

  • Fully online
  • Self-paced
  • Flexible scheduling

This makes it suitable for:

  • Working professionals
  • Students
  • Freelancers
  • Entrepreneurs

Financial Aid Availability

Coursera indicates that financial aid is available for eligible learners.

This creates additional accessibility opportunities for developers worldwide who may not be able to afford paid online learning programs.

Why Developers Should Consider This Opportunity

This specialization is particularly valuable because it focuses on practical AI engineering rather than only theoretical AI concepts.

Participants gain:

  • Portfolio-ready projects
  • Cloud deployment experience
  • AI agent development skills
  • Cost optimization strategies
  • Production engineering workflows

These capabilities align closely with the current demands of employers and AI-driven businesses globally.

Career Opportunities After Completion

Graduates of this specialization may pursue roles such as:

  • AI Application Developer
  • Generative AI Engineer
  • AI Solutions Architect
  • Prompt Engineer
  • AI Integration Specialist
  • Machine Learning Engineer
  • Backend AI Developer
  • AI Product Developer

As AI adoption accelerates globally, these career paths are expected to continue growing significantly.

Recently Updated in April 2026

The specialization was recently updated in April 2026, ensuring learners gain access to modern tools, current AI workflows, and the latest Gemini ecosystem capabilities.

This is particularly important in the rapidly evolving AI landscape where technologies and best practices change quickly.

How to Apply

Interested learners can enroll through Coursera and begin learning immediately at their own pace.

Applicants should prepare by:

  • Reviewing Python or JavaScript fundamentals
  • Understanding APIs and REST concepts
  • Setting up a development environment
  • Exploring basic AI concepts beforehand

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

The Google DeepMind Gemini for Developers Specialization is an outstanding opportunity for developers seeking to build practical, production-ready AI engineering skills in 2026.

With hands-on projects, cloud deployment training, AI agent development, and advanced Gemini API implementation, the specialization provides a strong pathway into the future of AI-powered software engineering.

For developers looking to remain competitive in the evolving global tech industry, this program offers highly relevant skills, industry-recognized certification, and practical portfolio-building experience.

Deadline

No application deadline was specified. Enrollment appears to be open on a rolling basis.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Professional Short Courses on OFY here.

The post Google DeepMind Gemini for Developers Specialization 2026: Become an AI App Builder with Gemini API and Google AI Studio appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity to Lead Change in Global Health Policy
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious and impactful program designed to empower passionate professionals to become leaders in global health advocacy, particularly in advancing surgical care access worldwide. This fellowship is offered through a powerful collaboration between Operation Smile, the University of Global Health Equity (UGHE), and the Nkafu Policy Institute—organizations deeply […]

The post Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity to Lead Change in Global Health Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship 2026 is a prestigious and impactful program designed to empower passionate professionals to become leaders in global health advocacy, particularly in advancing surgical care access worldwide. This fellowship is offered through a powerful collaboration between Operation Smile, the University of Global Health Equity (UGHE), and the Nkafu Policy Institute—organizations deeply committed to improving healthcare systems and transforming lives.

If you are driven to influence policy, create social change, and champion equitable surgical care, this fellowship provides a unique platform to elevate your voice and impact.

About the Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship

The Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship is an intensive and purpose-driven program aimed at developing leaders who can advocate for improved access to safe, timely, and affordable surgical care globally.

Through this fellowship, participants will:

  • Gain advanced knowledge in global surgery policy and advocacy
  • Develop leadership and communication skills
  • Design and implement real-world advocacy projects
  • Collaborate with experts and institutions shaping global health systems

The program is rooted in the mission of expanding healthcare access and addressing disparities in surgical care, particularly in underserved communities.

Objectives of the Fellowship

The fellowship focuses on equipping participants with the tools and expertise needed to:

  1. Advocate for policy change that prioritizes surgical care in national and global health agendas
  2. Strengthen leadership capacity in global health advocacy
  3. Drive social impact through innovative advocacy projects
  4. Promote equitable healthcare systems worldwide

Application Details

To apply for the Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship, candidates are required to submit an online application including the following components:

1. Personal Information

  • Full name
  • Email address
  • Phone number
  • Gender

2. Educational Background

  • Highest qualification attained
  • Area of specialization
  • Nationality
  • Country of residence

3. Professional Information

  • Current employer or organization
  • Job position
  • Years of professional experience

Required Documents

Applicants must upload the following documents (PDF format, maximum size: 20 MB each):

1. Personal Statement (Maximum 1 page)

Your personal statement should clearly address:

  • Your motivation for applying
  • Your experience in advocacy, policy influence, or social change
  • Leadership roles you have held
  • How you plan to use the fellowship experience to advance surgical care advocacy

2. Advocacy Project Proposal (Maximum 1 page)

Provide a concise description of:

  • A global surgery advocacy project you intend to develop during the fellowship
  • How the project aligns with your past or current advocacy efforts

3. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

  • Include detailed professional experience
  • Provide 2 to 3 contactable references

APPLY HERE

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted on or before:

May 31, 2026

Late submissions will not be considered, and due to the high volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in this fellowship offers numerous benefits:

  • Global Exposure: Work alongside international experts and institutions
  • Leadership Development: Strengthen your advocacy and policy influence skills
  • Career Advancement: Position yourself as a leader in global health
  • Real Impact: Contribute to meaningful change in healthcare access
  • Networking Opportunities: Build connections with professionals in global health and policy

Final Thoughts

The Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship is more than just a program—it is a call to action for individuals who are passionate about reshaping the future of healthcare. If you have a vision for equitable surgical care and the drive to influence policy and systems, this fellowship could be your next big step.

Take advantage of this opportunity to amplify your impact and contribute to a world where everyone has access to essential surgical services.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Global Surgery Advocacy Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity to Lead Change in Global Health Policy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LEAP Africa Social Innovators Programme Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded 9-Month Accelerator for Young African Entrepreneurs Driving Social Impact
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
The LEAP Africa Social Innovators Programme (SIP) Fellowship 2026 is now open for applications, offering a transformative opportunity for young African social entrepreneurs to scale their impact, access funding, and build sustainable enterprises. This prestigious 9-month fellowship programme is designed to empower early-stage innovators who are tackling pressing social challenges across Africa through entrepreneurship, innovation, […]

The post LEAP Africa Social Innovators Programme Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded 9-Month Accelerator for Young African Entrepreneurs Driving Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The LEAP Africa Social Innovators Programme (SIP) Fellowship 2026 is now open for applications, offering a transformative opportunity for young African social entrepreneurs to scale their impact, access funding, and build sustainable enterprises.

This prestigious 9-month fellowship programme is designed to empower early-stage innovators who are tackling pressing social challenges across Africa through entrepreneurship, innovation, and systems change.

With over a decade of proven impact, the SIP Fellowship continues to shape the next generation of African leaders by providing structured training, mentorship, funding access, and high-level exposure to investors and policymakers.

About the Social Innovators Programme (SIP)

The Social Innovators Programme (SIP) is an accelerator fellowship launched by LEAP Africa to strengthen the capacity of young African changemakers building social enterprises.

Programme Purpose

  • Address systemic challenges across Africa through innovation
  • Support early-stage social enterprises with tools for growth
  • Bridge gaps in funding, knowledge, and market access
  • Promote sustainable development and inclusive economic growth

Programme Impact and Legacy

Over the past 13 years, the SIP Fellowship has achieved remarkable results:

  • Over 294 social innovators trained and supported
  • Impacting more than 4 million lives across Africa
  • Fellows have generated over $7.8 million in funding and revenue
  • Active presence across 14 African countries, including:
    • Ghana
    • Kenya
    • Nigeria
    • Rwanda
    • Uganda
    • Tanzania
    • Sierra Leone
    • Cameroon
    • Malawi
    • Burundi
    • South Sudan
    • Gambia

This track record demonstrates the programme’s effectiveness in nurturing impactful and scalable social enterprises.

Fellowship Structure and Duration

The SIP Fellowship is a 9-month programme structured to provide both theoretical and practical support:

1. Intensive Virtual Training (4–6 Weeks)

Participants undergo structured sessions covering critical areas of enterprise development.

2. Mentorship Phase (4 Months)

Fellows are paired with experienced mentors and industry experts who provide guidance and support.

3. Practical Application and Growth

Participants apply learnings directly to their ventures, improving operations, strategy, and impact.

4. Physical Convergence and Awards Ceremony

The programme concludes with an in-person event where fellows:

  • Present their ventures to investors and stakeholders
  • Participate in the Social Innovators Programme Awards (SIPA)

Core Learning Areas

The fellowship curriculum is designed around key pillars essential for scaling social enterprises:

  1. Social innovation and enterprise design
  2. Market access and growth strategy
  3. Funding models and capital strategy
  4. Financial management and controls
  5. Impact measurement and outcome harvesting
  6. Strategy, governance, and team effectiveness
  7. Pitch development and stakeholder engagement

Key Benefits of the SIP Fellowship

Participants in the programme will gain access to:

  • Capacity-building through a structured curriculum
  • Mentorship from experienced professionals and industry experts
  • Exposure to investors, funders, and policymakers
  • Opportunities for funding and partnership development
  • Networking with a Pan-African community of innovators
  • Recognition through the SIP Awards

Social Innovators Programme Awards (SIPA)

The fellowship culminates in the SIP Awards, where outstanding participants are recognized.

Award Categories

  • Outstanding Fellow Awards
    Awarded to top-performing fellows demonstrating significant growth
  • Mr. Seyi Bickersteth Prize
    Recognizes excellence in financial accountability and sustainability
  • Mr. Innocent Chukwuma Prize
    Awarded to alumni contributing to gender empowerment and youth development

Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for the SIP Fellowship 2026, applicants must:

  • Be between 18 and 35 years old
  • Be a citizen of an African country
  • Be leading or working on a social enterprise addressing local challenges
  • Demonstrate commitment to social impact and innovation

Focus Areas for Social Enterprises

Applicants can come from a wide range of sectors, including:

  • Agriculture
  • Technology
  • Climate action
  • Sustainable cities and communities
  • Affordable and clean energy
  • Workforce development

Why You Should Apply

The SIP Fellowship offers a unique opportunity to:

  • Scale your social enterprise sustainably
  • Gain access to funding and investors
  • Build strategic partnerships across Africa
  • Enhance leadership and entrepreneurial skills
  • Join a network of high-impact African changemakers

How to Apply

Interested applicants should register via the official LEAP Africa platform.

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure their ventures align with the programme’s mission of driving social impact across Africa.

CLICK TO REGISTER 

Final Thoughts

The LEAP Africa Social Innovators Programme Fellowship 2026 is more than a training programme—it is a launchpad for Africa’s next generation of impact-driven entrepreneurs. By equipping young innovators with the tools, networks, and resources needed to succeed, SIP continues to play a vital role in shaping the continent’s future.

For more information on this fellowship visit HERE

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on HERE

The post LEAP Africa Social Innovators Programme Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded 9-Month Accelerator for Young African Entrepreneurs Driving Social Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Technical Advisor Job Opportunity in Malawi 2026: Join WE4D Africa Green Jobs Initiative with GIZ to Drive Women’s Employment and Sustainable Development
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
A compelling career opportunity has opened for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development, gender equality, and green economic transformation in Africa. The Technical Advisor position under the WE4D Malawi project, implemented by GIZ, offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of private sector development, green jobs creation, and women’s economic empowerment. […]

The post Technical Advisor Job Opportunity in Malawi 2026: Join WE4D Africa Green Jobs Initiative with GIZ to Drive Women’s Employment and Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
A compelling career opportunity has opened for experienced professionals seeking to contribute to sustainable development, gender equality, and green economic transformation in Africa. The Technical Advisor position under the WE4D Malawi project, implemented by GIZ, offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of private sector development, green jobs creation, and women’s economic empowerment.

This role is based in Lilongwe, Malawi, and is part of the broader regional program “Employment Promotion for Women for the Green Transformation in Africa (WE4D)”, which is transforming labor markets across multiple African countries.

About the WE4D Program

The WE4D program is a regional initiative designed to enhance employment opportunities for women, particularly within green sectors and emerging value chains driving Africa’s sustainable transformation.

Key Objectives of WE4D

  • Promote market-oriented education and skills development
  • Improve school-to-work transitions
  • Foster entrepreneurship and MSME competitiveness
  • Support job creation in green sectors
  • Strengthen inclusive labor markets, especially for women, youth, and Persons with Disabilities

Geographic Scope

The program operates across:

  • Cameroon
  • Kenya
  • Malawi
  • Mozambique
  • Senegal
  • South Africa
  • Tanzania
  • Togo
  • Uganda

Program Partners

  • Commissioned by: German Federal Ministry for Economic Cooperation and Development (BMZ)
  • Co-funded by: Norway and the European Union (EU)
  • Implemented by: Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH

Position Overview: Technical Advisor (WE4D Malawi)

  • Job Title: Technical Advisor
  • Project: WE4D Malawi
  • Duty Station: Lilongwe, Malawi
  • Reporting To: Program Component Manager
  • Application Deadline: 11 May 2026

This role focuses on delivering strategic and technical support in private sector development, communication, and knowledge management to advance green employment opportunities for women.

Key Responsibilities

1. Private Sector Development

The Technical Advisor will play a critical role in strengthening green job ecosystems by:

  • Managing partnership projects and interventions focused on:
    • Green skills development
    • Entrepreneurship support
    • Job matching initiatives
  • Promoting collaboration between:
    • Private sector actors
    • Training institutions
    • Government agencies
    • Civil society organizations
  • Designing and implementing strategies that:
    • Support women-led MSMEs
    • Enhance women’s access to green jobs
    • Promote inclusion of Persons with Disabilities
  • Coordinating enterprise development partnerships, including:
    • Workshop facilitation
    • Technical documentation
    • Monitoring and evaluation of project outcomes
  • Identifying and leveraging strategic partnerships across development actors

2. Communication and Knowledge Management

The role also involves strengthening visibility and communication through:

  • Developing and implementing internal and external communication strategies
  • Managing communication service providers and consultants
  • Producing high-quality communication materials and knowledge products
  • Organizing outreach events, field visits, and PR activities
  • Maintaining databases of communication assets and project documentation
  • Supporting knowledge-sharing platforms and regional communication initiatives

Core Tasks and Functions

The Technical Advisor will:

  1. Plan and execute entrepreneurship promotion strategies in green sectors
  2. Manage consultancies, including:
    • Terms of Reference development
    • Consultant selection and supervision
    • Quality assurance of deliverables
  3. Monitor program progress and contribute to reporting systems
  4. Facilitate workshops, stakeholder meetings, and collaboration platforms
  5. Support gender-responsive and inclusive program implementation

Required Qualifications and Competencies

Educational Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent in:
    • Business Administration
    • Agriculture
    • Gender Studies
    • Communications
    • Public Relations
    • Technical Education
    • Or a related field
  • Additional qualifications in development cooperation, TVET, or private sector development are an advantage

Professional Experience

  • Minimum of 5 years of relevant experience in:
    • Employment promotion
    • Private sector development
    • Women’s economic empowerment
  • Experience in:
    • Partnerships with private sector and training institutions
    • International development cooperation
    • Green sectors (renewable energy, circular economy, sustainable agriculture)
  • Proven expertise in:
    • Gender equality initiatives
    • Stakeholder engagement
    • Workshop facilitation and moderation

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English and Chichewa

Key Skills and Competencies

  • Strong communication and writing skills
  • Strategic thinking and advisory capacity
  • Networking and partnership-building ability
  • Ability to work independently and in multicultural teams
  • High level of initiative and proactiveness
  • Strong attention to visual and communication aesthetics

Why This Role Matters

This position offers an opportunity to directly contribute to:

  • Advancing gender equality and women’s economic empowerment
  • Supporting Africa’s green transformation agenda
  • Building sustainable and inclusive labor markets
  • Strengthening private sector-driven development

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through the official GIZ recruitment platform.

Interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the deadline of 11 May 2026.

APPLY HERE

Equal Opportunity Statement

GIZ is committed to diversity and inclusion and encourages applications from individuals of all backgrounds, including women and persons with disabilities.

For more information of this job visit HERE

Discover more career opportunities on OFY on HERE

The post Technical Advisor Job Opportunity in Malawi 2026: Join WE4D Africa Green Jobs Initiative with GIZ to Drive Women’s Employment and Sustainable Development appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Leader, Africa’s Food Future Initiative – Senior Conservation Job Opportunity at WWF (Kenya, 2026)
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) is offering an exceptional senior-level career opportunity for experienced professionals in sustainability, agriculture, and conservation. The Leader, Africa’s Food Future Initiative (AFFI) position, based in Nairobi, Kenya, represents a strategic leadership role aimed at transforming Africa’s food systems toward sustainability, resilience, and environmental integrity. This opportunity is ideal […]

The post Leader, Africa’s Food Future Initiative – Senior Conservation Job Opportunity at WWF (Kenya, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The World Wide Fund for Nature (WWF) is offering an exceptional senior-level career opportunity for experienced professionals in sustainability, agriculture, and conservation. The Leader, Africa’s Food Future Initiative (AFFI) position, based in Nairobi, Kenya, represents a strategic leadership role aimed at transforming Africa’s food systems toward sustainability, resilience, and environmental integrity.

This opportunity is ideal for seasoned professionals seeking to contribute to high-impact environmental initiatives while working at the intersection of agriculture, climate change, biodiversity conservation, and international development.

 About Africa’s Food Future Initiative (AFFI)

The Africa’s Food Future Initiative (AFFI) is a flagship program under WWF’s broader Roadmap 2030, which seeks to build a future where food systems are environmentally sustainable, economically viable, and socially inclusive.

With increasing pressure on natural ecosystems due to unsustainable agricultural practices and rising food demand, AFFI focuses on developing nature-positive agriculture models that can be scaled across the continent.

By 2030, AFFI aims to:

  • Establish sustainable and productive food systems across key African landscapes
  • Strengthen resilience among smallholder farming communities
  • Promote biodiversity conservation while ensuring food security
  • Build inclusive and fair agricultural value chains

 Key Geographic Focus Areas

The initiative is anchored in three high-biodiversity landscapes:

  • Kavango-Zambezi Transfrontier Conservation Area (KAZA)
  • Southern Kenya–Northern Tanzania (SOKNOT)
  • The Congo Basin (specific focal landscape to be determined)

These regions are critical for biodiversity conservation and are central to WWF’s strategy for sustainable land and food system transformation.

 Role Overview: Strategic Leadership for Sustainable Food Systems

As the Leader of AFFI, you will guide WWF country offices in delivering impactful programs that align with global sustainability targets. This includes driving innovation, fostering partnerships, and ensuring the successful implementation of nature-positive agricultural systems.

You will work closely with multidisciplinary teams across countries, influencing policies, mobilizing funding, and representing WWF in high-level forums across Africa.

 Key Responsibilities

Strategic Leadership & Programme Delivery

  • Lead implementation of AFFI across priority landscapes
  • Align national strategies with WWF’s global Food and Agriculture roadmap
  • Promote nature-positive agricultural transformation

Partnerships & Collaboration

  • Work with governments, NGOs, private sector actors, and donors
  • Coordinate multidisciplinary teams across regions

Policy & Advocacy

  • Provide intellectual leadership on sustainable agriculture policies
  • Influence regional and global policy frameworks

Resource Mobilization

  • Lead fundraising efforts and track funding streams
  • Support innovative financing mechanisms, including blended finance

Technical Support & Capacity Building

  • Guide country offices on agroecology, land-use planning, and value chains
  • Strengthen institutional capacity and safeguard policies

Communication & Representation

  • Represent WWF in high-level regional events
  • Collaborate with communications teams for outreach and visibility

 Required Qualifications and Experience

To be competitive for this role, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Minimum 10 years of professional experience, including 5 years in senior management roles
  • Master’s degree (or higher) in:
    • Agriculture
    • International Development
    • Natural Resource Management
    • Biodiversity Conservation
    • Business Administration or related fields
  • Strong expertise in:
    • Sustainable agriculture or agroecology
    • Land-use planning
    • Agricultural value chains
  • Demonstrated experience in:
    • Project and programme management
    • Working within global organizations
    • Engaging donors, governments, and private sector leaders
  • Excellent communication skills in English (French is an advantage)

 Key Skills and Competencies

  • Strategic thinking and leadership
  • High-level negotiation and diplomacy
  • Proposal writing, budgeting, and financial management
  • Ability to work across multicultural and virtual teams
  • Passion for conservation and social impact

 Working Environment and Collaboration

The selected candidate will work closely with:

  • WWF Country Directors and Conservation Managers
  • Global Food & Agriculture Practice teams
  • Regional programme leaders
  • External stakeholders including governments, NGOs, investors, and academic institutions

This role requires a high level of coordination across multiple countries and stakeholders, making it both challenging and highly impactful.

 Location

  • Nairobi, Kenya (WWF-Kenya Office)

 Application Deadline

15 May 2026

Applications submitted after this date will not be considered.

 Why This Opportunity Matters

This role is more than just a job—it is a chance to shape the future of food systems in Africa. By leading AFFI, you will directly contribute to building sustainable agricultural models that protect ecosystems, empower communities, and address climate challenges.

For more opportunities click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Leader, Africa’s Food Future Initiative – Senior Conservation Job Opportunity at WWF (Kenya, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Tufts University CEVIHE Postdoctoral Fellowship in Social Sciences 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for Early-Career Scholars
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Center for Expanding Viewpoints in Higher Education (CEVIHE) at Tufts University has announced applications for its Postdoctoral Fellow in the Social Sciences position for Fall 2026. This prestigious academic opportunity is designed for early-career scholars whose research explores difficult, contested, and consequential questions shaping public life, public policy, institutions, and society. The fellowship provides […]

The post Tufts University CEVIHE Postdoctoral Fellowship in Social Sciences 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Center for Expanding Viewpoints in Higher Education (CEVIHE) at Tufts University has announced applications for its Postdoctoral Fellow in the Social Sciences position for Fall 2026. This prestigious academic opportunity is designed for early-career scholars whose research explores difficult, contested, and consequential questions shaping public life, public policy, institutions, and society.

The fellowship provides scholars with a unique chance to combine research, university-level teaching, interdisciplinary collaboration, and public engagement while contributing to conversations around pluralism, open inquiry, and intellectual diversity in higher education.

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis beginning May 4, 2026, and the position remains open until filled.

About the CEVIHE Fellowship at Tufts University

The Center for Expanding Viewpoints in Higher Education (CEVIHE) seeks to support emerging scholars who are interested in studying how societies navigate disagreement around political, cultural, social, and moral questions.

This fellowship focuses particularly on research that investigates:

  • Controversial or contested issues in public life
  • Political and social disagreement
  • Public policy debates
  • Institutional and cultural conflict
  • The emergence and influence of controversial ideas or movements
  • The dynamics of ideological inclusion or marginalization
  • Open inquiry and pluralism in academic and civic spaces

The fellowship is one of the final two searches completing CEVIHE’s Fall 2026 postdoctoral fellowship cohort.

Host Institution: Tufts University

Founded in 1852, Tufts University is recognized internationally for:

  • High-quality teaching
  • Advanced basic and applied research
  • Global citizenship and civic engagement
  • Interdisciplinary learning
  • Commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion

The university encourages faculty and scholars to engage constructively with individuals from diverse cultural, ideological, and social backgrounds.

Fellowship Position Overview

Position Title

Postdoctoral Scholar – Social Sciences

Location

Somerville, Massachusetts, USA

Work Mode

On-site

Duration

  • Initial appointment: 1 year
  • Start date: July 1, 2026
  • Possible extension based on:
    • Performance
    • Funding availability
    • Mutual agreement

Work Schedule

  • 35 hours per week
  • Fellows are expected to work in person at least four days weekly

Salary

  • Hiring range: USD $65,000 – $75,000
  • Compensation depends on qualifications and experience

What Research Areas Are Encouraged?

CEVIHE welcomes applications from scholars whose work addresses underexamined or emerging questions in the social sciences.

Relevant research themes may include:

1. Political and Social Polarization

Research examining:

  • Ideological conflict
  • Political identity
  • Public disagreement
  • Democratic participation

2. Controversial Public Questions

Studies focused on:

  • Cultural disputes
  • Moral controversies
  • Institutional conflicts
  • Public policy debates

3. Influence and Marginalization of Ideas

Research investigating:

  • Why some perspectives gain influence
  • How certain viewpoints become marginalized
  • Institutional gatekeeping
  • Media ecosystems and public discourse

4. Open Inquiry and Academic Freedom

Projects exploring:

  • Freedom of expression
  • Intellectual diversity
  • Debate culture
  • University environments

5. Institutional and Civic Dynamics

Research on:

  • Citizens and civic engagement
  • Social movements
  • Organizations and public institutions
  • Trust in institutions

Eligible Academic Disciplines

Applicants may come from various social science fields, including:

  • Political Science
  • Sociology
  • Economics
  • Psychology
  • Communications
  • Public Policy
  • Media Studies
  • Related interdisciplinary social science disciplines

Interdisciplinary scholars are strongly encouraged to apply.

Teaching Responsibilities

The selected fellow will develop and teach two university courses connected to their research expertise.

Possible course formats include:

  • Seminars
  • Research-focused classes
  • Debate-centered discussion courses
  • Courses examining empirical and normative claims
  • Public policy analysis classes

The fellowship particularly values courses that help students:

  • Engage with competing ideas
  • Analyze evidence critically
  • Understand multiple perspectives
  • Evaluate contentious public questions thoughtfully

Courses may also be co-listed with other departments at Tufts University.

Research and Professional Development Opportunities

The fellowship offers significant support for independent academic growth.

Selected fellows will have opportunities to:

Conduct Independent Research

Develop original scholarly work while affiliated with CEVIHE.

Participate in Interdisciplinary Collaboration

Work alongside postdoctoral fellows from multiple departments and academic backgrounds.

Receive Faculty Mentorship

Each fellow will be mentored by two faculty members affiliated with CEVIHE.

Join Academic Programming

Participate in:

  • Weekly lunch series
  • Workshops
  • Seminars
  • Intellectual discussions
  • Public events

Build Teaching Experience

Gain valuable classroom and curriculum-development experience in a supportive university setting.

What Makes This Fellowship Unique?

This fellowship stands out because it directly supports scholarship focused on:

  • Serious engagement with competing viewpoints
  • Constructive disagreement
  • Intellectual pluralism
  • Open inquiry in higher education
  • Difficult conversations in public life

Rather than avoiding contentious issues, the fellowship encourages rigorous and evidence-based examination of them.

This makes it especially attractive for scholars studying:

  • Political conflict
  • Social polarization
  • Institutional trust
  • Media influence
  • Public discourse
  • Democracy and civic life

Required Application Materials

Applicants must submit the following documents:

Mandatory Documents

  1. Application Letter
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Brief Research Statement
  4. Teaching Philosophy Statement
  5. Proposed Teaching Contribution
  6. At Least One Sample Syllabus
  7. Two Confidential Letters of Reference

Optional Documents

  • Teaching evaluations (if available)

Reference letters must be submitted directly by the referees.

Application Review Timeline

Deadline Information

  • Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis beginning May 4, 2026
  • The position remains open until filled

Because the review process is rolling, early application submission is strongly recommended.

Contact Information

Questions regarding the fellowship may be directed to:

Arik Burakovsky
Email: Arik.Burakovsky@tufts.edu

Reference letters should also be submitted to this email address.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should:

  • Hold a Ph.D. in a relevant field by the appointment start date
  • Demonstrate strong research potential
  • Show teaching capability and interest
  • Have expertise related to contested public questions
  • Be able to teach students to evaluate competing ideas rigorously

Strong candidates will demonstrate:

  • Analytical rigor
  • Methodological clarity
  • Interdisciplinary engagement
  • Commitment to pluralism and open inquiry

Why Scholars Should Consider Applying

This fellowship offers an outstanding platform for early-career academics who want to:

  • Build a strong research profile
  • Gain university teaching experience
  • Engage deeply with public issues
  • Join interdisciplinary intellectual communities
  • Explore complex societal debates in a rigorous environment

The combination of research freedom, mentorship, teaching opportunities, and institutional support makes this a highly valuable academic opportunity.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the Tufts University CEVIHE Postdoctoral Fellowship.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Tufts University CEVIHE Postdoctoral Fellowship in Social Sciences 2026: Fully Detailed Guide for Early-Career Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: PhD Call 2026 at the University of Udine, Italy – Fully Detailed Guide for International Applicants
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The University of Udine has officially opened applications for its PhD Programmes – 42nd Cycle (2026), Selection Period 1, offering a remarkable opportunity for students worldwide to pursue advanced research in one of Europe’s most vibrant academic environments. Located in Udine, this institution is known for its strong emphasis on innovation, interdisciplinary research, and global […]

The post PhD Call 2026 at the University of Udine, Italy – Fully Detailed Guide for International Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Udine has officially opened applications for its PhD Programmes – 42nd Cycle (2026), Selection Period 1, offering a remarkable opportunity for students worldwide to pursue advanced research in one of Europe’s most vibrant academic environments. Located in Udine, this institution is known for its strong emphasis on innovation, interdisciplinary research, and global collaboration.

This comprehensive guide covers everything you need to know about the PhD call, including available programmes, eligibility expectations, application process, deadlines, and expert tips to strengthen your application.

Overview of the PhD Call 2026

The 42nd cycle of doctoral programmes at the University of Udine is designed to attract talented researchers from around the globe. The university fosters a collaborative academic culture, providing access to experienced supervisors, cutting-edge research facilities, and international partnerships.

Applicants will be evaluated through a competitive selection process, ensuring that only highly motivated and academically strong candidates are admitted.

Available PhD Programmes (12 Fields of Study)

The university offers a diverse range of doctoral programmes spanning both STEM and humanities disciplines:

Science, Technology & Engineering

  • Computer Science and Artificial Intelligence
  • Industrial and Information Engineering
  • Energy and Environmental Engineering Sciences
  • Agricultural Sciences and Biotechnology
  • Mathematical and Physical Sciences

Medical & Health Sciences

  • Molecular Medicine
  • Clinical and Translational Medical Sciences

Social Sciences, Humanities & Arts

  • Law for Innovation in the European Legal Area
  • Art History, Cinema, Audiovisual Media and Music
  • Linguistics and Literature
  • Historical and Philosophical Studies

Interdisciplinary & Applied Sciences

  • Food Sciences

These programmes are structured to encourage interdisciplinary research and innovation, allowing candidates to work on impactful global challenges.

Application Timeline

It is crucial to respect the official timeline, as late submissions are not accepted:

  • Opening Date: 30 April 2026 (14:00 – Italian Time)
  • Application Deadline: 4 June 2026 (14:00 – Italian Time)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to prepare their documents well in advance to avoid last-minute issues.

Required Application Documents

To successfully apply, candidates must submit a complete application through the official online system. Required documents include:

  1. Research Proposal
    • A well-structured and original proposal aligned with your chosen PhD programme.
  2. Academic Certificates
    • Self-certified copies of degrees and transcripts.
  3. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
    • Updated and tailored to highlight academic achievements, research experience, and relevant skills.
  4. Self-Certification Forms
    • As required by the university’s application guidelines.
  5. Optional: Interview Request
    • Candidates may request a videoconference interview if applicable.

Ensure all documents are accurate, complete, and submitted before the deadline.

Selection Process

Admission to the programme is competitive and based on a holistic evaluation of each candidate:

  • Academic Background: Quality and relevance of previous studies
  • Research Proposal: Originality, feasibility, and alignment with the programme
  • Interview Performance: Conducted if required

Final rankings and available positions are detailed in the official call documents.

Why Choose the University of Udine for Your PhD?

Choosing the right institution for your doctoral studies is critical. Here’s why this opportunity stands out:

  • International Research EnvironmentEngage with a global academic community and collaborate with researchers worldwide.
  • Expert SupervisionWork under experienced professors and researchers in your field.
  • Wide Range of DisciplinesFlexibility to pursue both technical and non-technical research interests.
  • Strategic Location in EuropeStudy in Italy while benefiting from proximity to major European research hubs.
  • Career Advancement OpportunitiesGain valuable research experience that enhances academic and professional prospects.

How to Apply

 Make sure to carefully review the official call document for programme-specific requirements and available positions.

Apply HERE

Final Tip for Applicants

A strong research proposal is the cornerstone of your application. Focus on clarity, originality, and relevance to the chosen programme. Demonstrating a clear research direction and understanding of your field can significantly increase your chances of selection.

For more information, visit official website

Discover more global PhD opportunities on OFY, VISIT

The post PhD Call 2026 at the University of Udine, Italy – Fully Detailed Guide for International Applicants appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026–2027 NABJ–Apple News Fellowship: Launch Your Career at the Intersection of Journalism and Technology
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The 2026–2027 NABJ–Apple News Fellowship is a highly competitive and career-defining opportunity for aspiring journalists who want to work at the cutting edge of digital media. This prestigious program is a collaboration between Apple Inc. and the National Association of Black Journalists, designed to empower early-career journalists with real-world newsroom experience, mentorship, and exposure to […]

The post 2026–2027 NABJ–Apple News Fellowship: Launch Your Career at the Intersection of Journalism and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026–2027 NABJ–Apple News Fellowship is a highly competitive and career-defining opportunity for aspiring journalists who want to work at the cutting edge of digital media. This prestigious program is a collaboration between Apple Inc. and the National Association of Black Journalists, designed to empower early-career journalists with real-world newsroom experience, mentorship, and exposure to innovative storytelling formats.

Apple News serves millions of readers worldwide by delivering high-quality journalism from leading global publishers. Through this fellowship, participants will step into a dynamic editorial environment where technology and storytelling meet to shape how audiences consume news today.

Why This Fellowship Matters

This program is more than just a work placement. It is a transformative learning experience that allows fellows to:

  • Work alongside top editors and media professionals
  • Gain firsthand experience in a globally recognized digital newsroom
  • Develop editorial judgment and storytelling skills
  • Understand audience engagement through data and metrics
  • Build a strong professional network in journalism and media

The fellowship is structured to ensure both breadth and depth of learning, making it ideal for individuals committed to a long-term career in digital journalism.

Fellowship Structure and Experience

The NABJ–Apple News Fellowship runs from August 2026 to May 2027, offering a structured yet flexible learning journey:

1. Editorial Rotations (First 3 Months)

Fellows will rotate across key editorial teams within Apple News, including:

  • Breaking news
  • Audio storytelling
  • Newsletters
  • Content curation

This rotation phase ensures exposure to different storytelling formats and editorial workflows.

2. Specialization Phase (Remaining 6 Months)

After rotations, fellows will:

  • Select a focus area in collaboration with Apple News leadership
  • Build expertise in a specific editorial function
  • Contribute meaningfully to ongoing editorial projects

Key Responsibilities

During the fellowship, selected candidates will actively contribute to the newsroom. Responsibilities include:

  • Curating high-quality content with accuracy and context
  • Writing compelling and engaging display copy
  • Planning editorial coverage for major news events
  • Collaborating with publisher partners and editors
  • Ensuring content meets editorial standards and guidelines
  • Analyzing performance metrics and improving engagement strategies
  • Presenting insights and project outcomes to internal teams

This hands-on involvement ensures fellows are not just observers but active contributors.

Mentorship and Professional Development

A major highlight of the program is its strong mentorship component:

  • Weekly one-on-one meetings with a manager
  • Dedicated mentorship support for career guidance
  • Continuous feedback to improve performance and skills

This structured support system helps fellows grow both professionally and personally.

Compensation and Work Environment

  • Pay: $45 per hour
  • Work Schedule: Up to 40 hours per week
  • Work Mode: Hybrid (remote applicants are also considered)

This ensures financial support while maintaining flexibility for participants.

Eligibility Requirements

To qualify for the fellowship, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  1. Be a current NABJ member in good standing
  2. Be legally authorized to work in the United States
  3. Be a recent graduate (by June 2026) or have less than three years of journalism experience
  4. Hold a minimum GPA of 3.0 (on a 4.0 scale)
  5. Have a degree in journalism, communications, or a related field (or relevant experience)
  6. Demonstrate strong editorial skills and news judgment
  7. Show commitment to a career in digital journalism
  8. Complete the official application process

Applicants who are currently employed must also obtain approval from their employer.

Important Deadline

The application deadline is May 29, 2026, at 11:59 PM (PT). Late submissions will not be considered, so early application is strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should:

  • Join NABJ if not already a member
  • Prepare academic transcripts and supporting documents
  • Complete the official application form
  • Submit all materials before the deadline

For inquiries, applicants can contact: awards@nabj.org

Final Thoughts

The NABJ–Apple News Fellowship stands out as a powerful launchpad for early-career journalists. It combines mentorship, real-world newsroom exposure, and cutting-edge digital media experience. If you are passionate about storytelling, technology, and shaping the future of journalism, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity to grow and succeed at a global level.

Discover more global fellowship opportunitieson OFY here

The post 2026–2027 NABJ–Apple News Fellowship: Launch Your Career at the Intersection of Journalism and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AIR–AERC 2026 Impact Evaluation Faculty Retooling Workshop: Strengthening Africa’s Research Capacity in Policy Evaluation
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The AIR–AERC 2026 Impact Evaluation Faculty Retooling Workshop is a high-level training opportunity designed to enhance the skills of African researchers and academic faculty in evaluating social and economic policies. Organized by the African Economic Research Consortium in collaboration with the American Institutes for Research, this program is part of a broader effort to strengthen […]

The post AIR–AERC 2026 Impact Evaluation Faculty Retooling Workshop: Strengthening Africa’s Research Capacity in Policy Evaluation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The AIR–AERC 2026 Impact Evaluation Faculty Retooling Workshop is a high-level training opportunity designed to enhance the skills of African researchers and academic faculty in evaluating social and economic policies. Organized by the African Economic Research Consortium in collaboration with the American Institutes for Research, this program is part of a broader effort to strengthen Africa’s capacity for evidence-based policymaking.

Established in 1988, AERC has played a pivotal role in advancing economic research and graduate training across Africa. Through strategic partnerships, policy engagement, and knowledge co-production, the organization continues to empower African scholars to address pressing economic challenges with rigor and independence.

Why This Workshop Matters

Across Africa, governments and development organizations invest heavily in social and economic programs. However, understanding whether these programs actually deliver measurable impact remains a critical challenge. This workshop directly addresses that gap by equipping participants with advanced tools and methodologies to evaluate program effectiveness.

Participants will gain the ability to answer key policy questions such as:

  • Does a specific program achieve its intended outcomes?
  • How does impact vary across different population groups?
  • Which interventions are most cost-effective?
  • What mechanisms drive program success or failure?

By strengthening these analytical capabilities, the workshop contributes to more effective policymaking and improved development outcomes across the continent.

Workshop Objectives

The training is structured to provide both theoretical knowledge and practical skills. Key objectives include:

  1. Developing a solid understanding of program and policy impact evaluation concepts
  2. Learning how to formulate and refine evaluation questions
  3. Exploring different evaluation designs and methodologies
  4. Building criteria for selecting appropriate estimation strategies
  5. Interpreting evaluation results accurately and linking them to policy implications
  6. Gaining hands-on experience with quantitative tools and real-world data

What Participants Will Learn

This intensive six-day, face-to-face workshop covers a wide range of advanced topics in impact evaluation, including:

  • Differences between impact evaluation and other evaluation approaches
  • Core principles of answering evaluation questions
  • Key challenges such as confounding variables, selectivity, and externalities
  • Experimental and quasi-experimental designs
  • Matching techniques and regression discontinuity methods
  • Instrumental variables and addressing endogeneity
  • Difference-in-differences estimation

The training combines lectures, case studies, and practical sessions using Stata, ensuring participants leave with actionable skills.

Training Approach and Facilitators

The workshop adopts a highly interactive and practical learning approach. Participants are encouraged to bring their own research projects or program evaluation cases for discussion.

Training will be delivered by experts from:

  • University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill
  • American Institutes for Research
  • UNICEF Office of Research – Innocenti

This diverse team of facilitators ensures participants benefit from global expertise and real-world insights.

Dates and Venue

The workshop will take place in Nairobi, Kenya, following this schedule:

  • June 28, 2026: Arrival of participants
  • June 29 – July 4, 2026: Full-day training sessions
  • July 5, 2026: Departure

The face-to-face format allows for deeper engagement, networking, and collaborative learning among participants from across Africa.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for this prestigious workshop, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a national of an African country
  • Hold a PhD in Economics, Agricultural Economics, or a related field
  • Be a teaching faculty member at an African university and/or an early-career researcher
  • Have a record of publications in peer-reviewed outlets
  • Demonstrate strong quantitative skills, including knowledge of regression analysis
  • Be proficient in computer use and familiar with household survey data

The course will be conducted in English, with French interpretation available to support wider participation.

Application Process

Applicants must submit their applications via email available here

Applications should include:

  • A cover letter clearly stating eligibility and interest
  • A recommendation letter from the Head of Department
  • A detailed Curriculum Vitae (including gender, nationality, qualifications, and publications)

All applications should be addressed to the Executive Director of AERC and clearly reference:
“Application for AIR-AERC 2026 Impact Evaluation Faculty Retooling Workshop”.

Important Deadline

The application deadline is Friday, May 15, 2026. Late applications will not be considered, so early submission is strongly advised.

Final Thoughts

The AIR–AERC 2026 Impact Evaluation Workshop represents a valuable opportunity for African scholars to deepen their expertise in policy evaluation. By combining rigorous academic training with practical application, the program equips participants with the tools needed to influence policy decisions and contribute to sustainable development across Africa.

Discover more global workshop opportunities available on OFY Here

The post AIR–AERC 2026 Impact Evaluation Faculty Retooling Workshop: Strengthening Africa’s Research Capacity in Policy Evaluation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
